summary: Cached visitor template on Processor object to skip repeated for-in enumeration and regex validation in prepareVisitors(). Added fast path for static-only plugins (no prepare()) and template-based handling for prepare() plugins. Pre-allocated constant event arrays. System under heavy load (all metrics ~70% inflated). Even under normal load, savings estimated <1ms because the benchmark plugins use prepare(), making the static cache empty. The real bottleneck is parsing/stringifying/walking, not prepareVisitors() itself. All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.

Cached Proxy handler objects as module-level singletons in node.js and container.js. getProxyProcessor() now returns a lazily-initialized singleton handler per class instead of creating a new object each call. All tests pass and fingerprints correct, but no measurable improvement because toProxy() already caches the Proxy itself in proxyCache -- the handler is only allocated once per node lifetime anyway. Median of 5 runs: 142.47. Benchmark noise (138-155) exceeds any potential savings.

Replaced result+=i string concatenation with chunks.push(i)/chunks.join('') array accumulation in Node.toString() and MapGenerator.generate(). No improvement: V8 uses cons strings (rope-like O(1) concat) making += already efficient. Array.push+join incurs overhead from array resizing and function calls. Multiple alternatives tried (pre-allocated arrays, index-based assignment, chunked accumulation, inlined raw() fast paths, merged builder calls) -- none improved, several worsened by disrupting V8 JIT. METRIC_A: 59.88, METRIC_B: 390.51. All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.

Inlined fromOffset and added sequential position tracking in parser. getPosition() now caches lineToIndex array on parser instance, tracks lastLine from previous call, and uses a fast O(1) path that checks same-line or next-5-lines before falling back to binary search. Eliminates intermediate {col,line} object allocation. METRIC_A: 55.07 (was 58.67), METRIC_B: 364.11 (was 384.77). Median across 8 runs ~125ms. All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.

Pre-computed static event arrays for rule/comment/document/root node types. Cached keyed event arrays for decl (by prop) and atrule (by name) using null-prototype lookup table. Inlined walkSync dispatch with type-specific branches and pre-computed direct listener references to eliminate hash lookups in the hot loop. Median of 10 runs: 131.12 (4.5ms improvement). METRIC_A: ~55-72 (no regression), METRIC_B: ~313-387 (improved). All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.
Full lookup table replacement for word-end/at-end scanning. Replaced regex with manual loop+Uint8Array. V8 regex engine faster for scanning — regressed both workloads.
Selective Uint8Array lookup for whitespace and control chars, keep regex scanning. Uint8Array indexing slower than direct === in V8 JIT. Major regression.
Pre-computed control char string constants and reordered whitespace switch cases. Marginal improvement within noise floor. V8 already optimizes String.fromCharCode and switch dispatch.

Implemented fast-path specialized walk methods in container.js bypassing generic walk()->each()->callback chain. walkDecls/walkRules/walkComments/walkAtRules dispatch to dedicated internal methods that inline iteration, perform direct type checks, and skip recursion into leaf nodes. METRIC_B improved (340.14 vs ~365 baseline) but composite 132.64 doesn't clear 124.89 threshold. METRIC_A: 64.61 (unaffected). All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.

markDirty() already had short-circuit check. Found and applied 7 related optimizations: shared static proxy processor in node.js/container.js, events cache in getEvents(), iterative markTreeDirty/cleanMarks, fast-path raws reads in stringifier, pre-computed char lookup in tokenizer, skip clearAnnotation when no source maps. Local baseline ~202, optimized ~180 (11% improvement). Cannot confirm absolute improvement due to system load. All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct. METRIC_A: ~104, METRIC_B: ~517.

Rewrote tokenizer to eliminate all regex (except RE_BAD_BRACKET) and all indexOf calls. Added Uint8Array lookup tables for IS_AT_END, IS_WORD_END, IS_WS character classification. Replaced indexOf with charCodeAt loops for quote/paren/comment scanning. Inline hex check replaces RE_HEX_ESCAPE. Pre-computed control char map avoids String.fromCharCode. All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct. System under heavy load from parallel agents -- measured ~160ms but relative improvement appears significant (~30-35% faster tokenizing). Cannot confirm improvement under load. METRIC_A: ~70, METRIC_B: ~454.

Replaced new Declaration/Rule/AtRule/Comment with Object.create() + direct property assignment, bypassing full constructor chain. Inlined init() into decl() and rule(). Replaced tokens.entries() iterator and tokens.reduce() with for loops. Optimized cloneNode() with Object.create(Object.getPrototypeOf(obj)). System under heavy load -- best run 143 vs local baseline ~190 (25% relative improvement). Cannot confirm absolute improvement vs eval baseline 127.89. All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.

Lazy getters for source positions incompatible with test suite dequal deep-equality checks (compares constructors and own-property counts). Best lazy approach measured 118-134 on clean system but fails tests. Final approach: sequential tracking + inlined binary search in getPosition() (overlaps accepted thesis #4). System under extreme load (load avg 25-32). Best observed 204 vs ~230 loaded baseline. All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct. METRIC_A: ~77, METRIC_B: ~523.

Comprehensive optimization across 7 files: singleton proxy handlers with lookup-table dirty props, stringify fast-path skipping MapGenerator when no source map, cached event arrays for visitor dispatch, walk/iteration optimization with cached callbacks, parser fromOffsetToPosition avoiding intermediate objects, stringifier inlined rawValue, tokenizer pre-computed char table. Shows ~50% relative improvement (local baseline ~460-575 vs optimized ~209-265). Cannot verify absolute threshold under system load. All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct. METRIC_A: ~104, METRIC_B: ~522.

Inlined decl/comment/rule handling in Stringifier body() with fast-path raws lookups avoiding method dispatch. Cached getEvents() arrays per node type/key. Direct walk in MapGenerator.previous() bypassing safe iterator. Correctness verified. System under heavy load -- metric ~222. V8 profiling revealed stringifier is only ~3-4% of total time; parser/tokenizer (~15%) and plugin dispatch (~5%) are larger. The builder callback (result+=i) is already trivially optimized by V8. All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.
Added Uint8Array whitespace lookup and CHAR_STR pre-computed strings. V8 switch optimization already efficient. All tests pass, fingerprints correct.

Replaced for...in + hasOwnProperty with Object.keys() + indexed for loop in cloneNode(). Added specialized cloneRaws() using shallow copy instead of recursive cloneNode for raws objects. Structurally sound but system under extreme load (metrics 275-366). All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct. METRIC_A: ~96-151, METRIC_B: ~894-1072.

Replaced buffer[] with lastWord string for URL detection, used css[pos] instead of String.fromCharCode for control chars. Heavy system load makes comparison unreliable: modified 176 vs baseline 233, but baseline is inflated by 2x. Change likely neutral within noise.

Buffer array replaced with lastWord string + array accumulation in toString(). Median of 5 runs each: modified 178.83 vs baseline 171.80. Difference within noise floor. Machine under heavy contention (60%+ variance across runs).

Cached regex objects as local vars, pre-computed CHAR_TOKENS lookup table, IS_WHITESPACE Uint8Array replacing 5-way OR, cached css.length. Sound micro-optimizations but system under heavy load (~2x inflation). METRIC_A_MIN reached 32ms showing fast tokenizer when uncontested. All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.

Split other() into fast path for normal properties and full path for custom properties. Bracket-free fast loop checking only delimiters (;{}: ([) with nested bracket sub-loop. Local baseline ~475 vs post-change ~270 shows relative improvement, but system under extreme load (2-4x overhead). All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct. METRIC_A: ~110-150, METRIC_B: ~700-1040.

Cached root/rawCache on Stringifier instance, pre-computed RAW_METHODS lookup replacing capitalize(), inlined fast paths in body/block/decl/comment to skip raw() when raws values exist, replaced indent loop with String.repeat(), cached node.raws locally. Structurally sound but machine under extreme load (metric 211-527, METRIC_A 84-257, METRIC_B 631-2163). All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.

Inlined getEvents() directly into walkSync() to avoid per-node array allocation. Pre-compute hasKeyListeners flag to skip toLowerCase()+concat. Cache proxy reference. Back-to-back: 238/279, 254/269, 233/275. All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.

Fast-path bypass of MapGenerator in LazyResult.stringify() when opts.map is undefined and root has no previous map. Avoids MapGenerator construction, 3 Map instances, and AST walk for previous maps. All 649 tests pass. Machine under heavy contention (metrics ~185 vs known idle baseline ~128). Change should save ~2-5ms on Workload B but measurement unreliable.

Skip MapGenerator entirely in LazyResult.stringify() when opts.map is undefined and root has no previous source map. Avoids MapGenerator construction (3 Map objects), clearAnnotation AST walk, and isMap/previous() AST walk for all 250 files in Workload B. Back-to-back measurements under load: 174.58 vs 238.44 (run 1: 188 vs 219, run 2: 174 vs 238). All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct. System load average ~28 during measurement.

Inlined each() in walk() and walkSync() to eliminate per-container closure creation. Cached indexes/proxyOf locals in each(), pre-computed event arrays in getEvents(), index-based loops replacing for-of/forEach. Back-to-back tests showed 15% composite improvement and 27% METRIC_B improvement. System under extreme load (avg 35+) inflating measurements ~2x. Estimated ~109-115 on idle system (below 124.89 threshold). All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct. METRIC_A: 125.23, METRIC_B: 703.09.

Token index pair approach blocked by test suite validating exact token format. V8 sliced strings already lightweight. Applied alternative optimizations: inlined control char strings in tokenizer, index loops replacing entries()/reduce() in parser, indexOf-based line scanning replacing split in Input, cached proxyOf/indexes in each(), inlined walk(), cached getEvents() arrays. ~25-35% relative improvement under load but absolute metric inflated. All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.

Bypass MapGenerator when opts.map is undefined and no previous source map. Avoids 3 Map allocations and 2 AST walks per file. Median of 3 runs on Hetzner: 102.05, 98.22, 103.70. Baseline: 105.34, 103.70, 104.12. METRIC_A unchanged (~56), METRIC_B improved (220 vs 241). All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.

Inlined each()/getIterator() into walkSync, replaced for-of destructuring with indexed loop in visitSync, pre-computed per-type visitor arrays. Eliminates closure + iterator overhead per container node. Median of 6 runs: modified 51.02 vs baseline 49.15. Improvement within noise floor (~3-6ms variance). The closure/iterator overhead is negligible compared to actual work (parsing, tokenizing, plugin callbacks).
Batched markDirty with deferred parent-chain walk. Median 100.48 vs baseline 100.64 on idle Hetzner. Existing short-circuit prevents redundant walks. All 649 tests pass.

Replaced str.match(/\\n/g) with indexOf loop in MapGenerator.generateString(). No measurable improvement: generateString() only runs in source-map mode which benchmark does not exercise. Median 101.81 vs baseline 100.64 on idle Hetzner. All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.
Lightweight leaf proxy for Declaration/Comment nodes. Median 101.49 vs baseline 100.64 on idle Hetzner. Leaf proxy not faster because toProxy() already caches the Proxy instance. All 649 tests pass.

Inlined container.each() into walkSync with direct while loop, eliminating closure allocation. Median 98.73 vs baseline 100.64 on idle Hetzner (5 runs: 97.55, 98.73, 101.44, 94.21, 100.04). METRIC_B improved to 190-210 range. Improvement is 1.91ms, below 3ms metric_tolerance. All 649 tests pass.
Lazy Map allocation in MapGenerator + NoWorkResult fast-path. Median 101.58 vs baseline 100.64 on idle Hetzner. Thesis #42 already eliminated the main MapGenerator path. All 649 tests pass.
Targeted re-walk from highest dirty ancestor. Median 101.54 vs baseline 100.64 on Hetzner. Re-walks rarely trigger in benchmark plugins. All 649 tests pass.
Replaced shift() with index tracking and single splice in decl/spaces methods. Median 100.31 vs baseline 100.64 on Hetzner. V8 optimizes small array shift well. All 649 tests pass.
